title: Build Your Own Forth Interpreter
summary: Tutorial on implementing a Forth interpreter from scratch. Forth is a stack-based language with unique characteristics. High translation value as it teaches core interpreter concepts (tokenization, stack operations, word definitions) applicable to language implementation generally. Well-structured educational content suitable for developers learning PL fundamentals.

title: OpenAI, the US government and Persona built an identity surveillance machine
summary: Investigative piece on identity verification infrastructure involving OpenAI, Persona, and government agencies. Covers KYC/identity systems and surveillance implications. Moderate-high technical depth on identity verification architecture. Timely topic on privacy-tech intersection worth translating for awareness.

title: Stripe reportedly makes offer to acquire PayPal
summary: Financial/business news about potential Stripe-PayPal acquisition. Low technical content - primarily market speculation and M&A reporting. Major business story but minimal technical value for developer audience. Not recommended for translation.

title: The Missing Semester of Your CS Education – Revised for 2026
summary: MIT's renowned practical CS skills course covering version control, debugging, build systems, and tooling. Updated 2026 edition. Exceptional translation value - teaches essential skills often missing from traditional CS curricula. Accessible to students and professionals. Educational content has highest ROI for translation.

title: FreeBSD doesn't have Wi-Fi driver for my old MacBook. AI build one for me
summary: Technical account of using AI to develop a Wi-Fi driver for FreeBSD. Combines kernel programming, reverse engineering, and AI-assisted development. High novelty - demonstrates AI tools applied to low-level systems work. Excellent translation value for systems programmers interested in practical AI integration.
